title: MiroFish Canvas Webapp Workbench
creator: Agent Buildprint
summary: Executable Buildprint remap of MiroFish into a source-independent graph/simulation/report-agent webapp workbench with proof-gated phases and provider/runtime evidence ceilings.
plainDescription: A Mapper OS executable packet for rebuilding the MiroFish canvas webapp workflow: seed upload, graph memory, simulation environment setup, dual-platform runtime, report-agent timeline, and deep interaction console.
promise: A source-independent, proof-gated implementation packet for the MiroFish workbench flow without claiming live-provider, deployment, security, or final product qualification before evidence exists.
whatYouGet: Five phase files with phase-preflight, claim typing, proof IDs, and repair routing
Evidence ledger schema and seed blocker row
whatYouNeed: Nothing for deterministic no-network proof mode
Optional AI/image/video provider keys only for live-provider upgrade proof
Security review before public deployment or destructive/admin claims
includes: 02-project-setup.md architecture, experience quality, Foundation scaffold gate, and ui-identity requirement
03-phases phase index, phase-flow, and five phase files
risks: Treating generated/agent-prompt.md as source of truth
Counting deterministic providers as live provider proof
Generic dashboard UI, bare graph demo, or static cards instead of a visually compelling storyboard-first workbench
Using Toonflow source as implementation input
checks: BUILDPRINT.md routes to blueprint.yaml, product-quality/UX gates, and active phase
02-project-setup.md requires implementation-project architecture.md, engineering-standards.md, test-strategy.md, and ui-identity.md
Each phase file has phase-flow entry, product outcome, mapped obligations, UI/UX contract, proof gate, and repair routing
Claim status remains PROOF_REQUIRED until browser, storyboard product quality, provider, persistence, security, and clean-room proof rows pass

trustBadges.label: Product-led packet
trustBadges.detail: Product brief, golden path, setup contract, phase files, acceptance gates, and evidence ledger are separated.

trustBadges.label: One-slice-at-a-time execution
trustBadges.detail: Agents load the active phase first and consult the phase index only after proof.

trustBadges.label: Clean-room boundary
trustBadges.detail: Derived from Toonflow evidence, but implementation must not use Toonflow source as input.

trustBadges.label: Proof required
trustBadges.detail: Runtime/provider/persistence/security/clean-room proof still gates any qualification claim.

copyPrompt: Use the Novel Storyboard Buildprint. First bootstrap exact snapshots: agb start https://agent-buildprint.com/buildprints/novel-storyboard/package.json . If agb is not installed, clone https://github.com/DomEscobar/agent-buildprint and run node agent-buildprint/bin/agb.js start https://agent-buildprint.com/buildprints/novel-storyboard/package.json . Then read .buildprint/next-agent.md and continue. Do not write Buildprint snapshots manually. The bootstrapped packet is the source of truth — follow its read order (BUILDPRINT.md, 01-questions.md, 02-project-setup.md, blueprint.yaml, 03-phases/phase-index.yaml, 04-evaluation.md, and the active phase files) instead of restating its rules here.
